mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized demand and employment growth, validating corporate revenue projections and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate capacity utilization and manufacturing demand, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 65% of observations during recovery regimes, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts breaching the 600,000-unit threshold indicates a structural turnaround in residential construction and related supply chains, lifting forward earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain negative in greater than 60% of months where housing starts exceed 600,000, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation raises expectations for monetary tightening and erodes real corporate cash flows, mechanically depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 55% of months with rising CPI, or the correlation between CPI growth and 20-day returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly industrial production growth confirm broad-based economic acceleration, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in greater than 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is subsumed by single-series predictors in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates surpassing 9 percent indicate severe labor market distress and collapsing household income, which directly suppresses consumer discretionary spending and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of months where unemployment exceeds 9 percent, or the predictive power disappears after controlling for monetary policy shocks.
